channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump warns of 'global catastrophe' following Iranian missile attack on Israel
item.description: Former President Trump on Tuesday bashed President Biden and Vice President Harris in the wake of an Iranian missile attack on Israel, arguing if Harris is elected in November it would lead to mass chaos on the world stage.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: What to know about Hezbollah's capabilities after its recent losses
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah has suffered some of the heaviest losses in its history over the past two weeks, chief among them the killing of its longtime leader, Hassan Nasrallah, in an Israeli airstrike.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How does Israel’s Iron Dome work?
item.description: Iran launched missiles toward Israel Tuesday, just days after Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah was killed in a strike on Lebanon. The Iranian blitz comes after a nearly yearlong cross-border conflict between Hezbollah, a star proxy group for Tehran, and Israeli forces.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Graham: Iranian missile attack on Israel deserves 'overwhelming response' from US
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) responded to Iran’s launch of a missile on Israel and said the attack deserves an “overwhelming response” from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels launch drone boat that hits ship in Red Sea as missile strikes another
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels launched an explosive-loaded drone that crashed into one ship Tuesday in the Red Sea and a missile that exploded against another.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Russian fighter jet flies within feet of US F-16 near Alaska
item.description: The U.S. military is slamming Russia for an “unsafe” encounter in the skies off the coast of Alaska after footage released Monday shows a Kremlin fighter jet cutting off an Air Force F-16. The incident, which took place Sept.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: US Embassy in Israel warns employees to shelter in place amid threat of Iran missile attack
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Jerusalem is cautioning all government employees and their family members to shelter in place until further notice as Iran prepares to launch missiles at Israel. The warning came as U.S. officials said they expected Iran to attack Israel with missile and drone strikes on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: NATO's new chief makes Ukraine support a top priority and says he will work with any US president
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The new head of NATO vowed on Tuesday to help shore up Western support for war-ravaged Ukraine and expressed confidence that he can work with whoever is elected president of the United States, the alliance's most powerful member, in November.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thailand school bus bursts into flames outside Bangkok, 23 feared dead, officials say
item.description: A school bus burst into flames on Tuesday while carrying more than 40 students and teachers on a field trip near Bangkok, Thailand, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WikiLeaks' Julian Assange says he pleaded 'guilty to journalism' in order to be freed
item.description: STRASBOURG, France (AP) — WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange said Tuesday that he was freed after years of incarceration because he "pled guilty to journalism.
item.dc:creator: PASCAL BASTIEN, BARBARA SURK and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A likely Russian artillery strike kills at least 6 at a Ukrainian market
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — An apparent Russian artillery strike hit a market in the southern Ukraine city of Kherson on Tuesday, killing at least six people and wounding three others, authorities said, on a morning when Ukrainians across the country were observing a minute’s silence for their military and
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwan shuts schools and offices ahead a direct hit from powerful typhoon
item.description: KAOHSIUNG, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan closed schools and offices and evacuated hundreds from vulnerable areas around the island Tuesday ahead of a strong typhoon expected to hit its densely populated western coast after lashing northern Philippine islands.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hezbollah terrorists engaged in sex slavery, rape, mass murder of Syrians
item.description: Startling new information has been released about Hezbollah’s enslavement and rapes of women amid Israel’s decapitation of senior Hezbollah terrorists over the last two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A former hostage fought for her own life in Gaza. A year on, she fights for her husband's freedom
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — As a hostage in Gaza, Aviva Siegel found herself begging for food and water. Since her release, she has found herself begging for her husband to be set free from his own ongoing captivity. Siegel has come to embody the disaster that befell Israel on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indian-controlled Kashmir votes in final phase of polls to elect local government
item.description: Voting has begun in Indian-controlled Kashmir in the final phase of an election to choose a local government.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Japan's new premier vows to push strong defense under Japan-US alliance and boost economy
item.description: The newly elected Japanese prime minister pledged to stick to the vital Japan-U.S. alliance amid growing tension in the region while calling for it to be more equitable.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel begins limited ground incursions in Lebanon
item.description: Israel has begun limited ground incursions into Lebanon for the first time, a dramatic escalation in the 11-month conflict with the Iranian-backed militia group Hezbollah and the start of a larger and potentially long fight against the group.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Israel launches limited ground operations in Lebanon as war against Hezbollah, terrorist groups continue
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) began limited operations in Lebanon on Monday evening, continuing its battle against Hezbollah terrorists. The military killed several key figures of the group over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN extends Haiti mission mandate after dropping push for larger peacekeeping effort
item.description: The United Nations Security Council on Monday unanimously renewed a multinational security support mission (MSS) in Haiti to combat armed gangs after the U.S. dropped a push to enlarge the effort into a peacekeeping force. The resolution, led by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Russian fighter jet intercepted near Alaska by NORAD forces after ‘unsafe, unprofessional’ interaction
item.description: North American Aerospace Defense Command (NORAD) released a video showing the dangerous moment a Russian Su-35 fighter pilot flew right in front of a NORAD F-16 fighter pilot over the Alaska Air Defense Zone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Flooding, landslides kill nearly 200 in Nepal
item.description: At least 193 people have been confirmed dead following severe flooding and landslides in Nepal, where heavy weekend rainfall was reported.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli raids against Hezbollah signal a wider ground incursion into Lebanon could come soon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Israeli military launched small ground raids against Hezbollah and sealed off communities along its northern border on Monday as Israeli artillery pounded southern Lebanon and signals grew that more forces could soon be sent across the border to fight the Iran-backed militants.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, SAMY MAGDY, ABBY SEWELL and NATALIE MELZER Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hezbollah says it will choose Nasrallah's successor 'at the earliest opportunity'
item.description: Hezbollah’s second-in-command, deputy leader Naim Qassem, says the terrorist group will choose its new leader "at the earliest opportunity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah's acting leader vows to fight on after Nasrallah's death
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah's acting leader vowed Monday to continue battling Israel and said the militant group was prepared for a long fight even after much of its top command was wiped out, including its leader, Hassan Nasrallah.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Who were the 7 high-ranking Hezbollah officials killed over the past week?
item.description: The move left Lebanon and much of the Mideast in shock as Israeli officials celebrated major military breakthroughs.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden says he will talk to Netanyahu as Israel pummels Sunni terror targets in Beirut
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces reportedly began striking the Lebanese capital of Beirut on Sunday evening after President Biden said he intended to speak with Prime Minister Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IDF announces death of another senior Hezbollah official following Nasrallah death
item.description: The Israeli military says it killed yet another member of Hezbollah's top command on Sunday, after wiping out leadership in a Friday strike.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Flooding and landslides in Nepal kill at least 66 people, with as many again still missing
item.description: KATHMANDU, Nepal (AP) — Flooding and landslides caused by continuous rainfall has killed at least 66 people in Nepal, and a further 69 are missing, officials said Saturday. Rain has been pouring down since Friday night and is expected to continue over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: BINAJ GURUBACHARYA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A far-right party is looking for a historic election win in Austria
item.description: Austria's far-right Freedom Party could win its first national election when the country votes on Sunday, but polls point to a close race in the parliamentary election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reward offered for Iranian in alleged Bolton assassination plot
item.description: Iranian Vice-President Mohammad Javad Zarif rejected accusations that Iran has tried to assassinate government critics overseas.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: US and allies call for an immediate 21-day cease-fire between Israel and Hezbollah
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S., France and other allies jointly called Wednesday for an immediate 21-day cease-fire to allow for negotiations in the escalating conflict between Israel and Hezbollah that has killed more than 600 people in Lebanon in recent days.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, MATTHEW LEE and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russian missiles and drones target Kyiv for 5 hours and hit Ukraine's power grid
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine’s air defenses battled an overnight Russian aerial attack on the capital Kyiv for five hours, officials said Thursday, as missiles and drones again hammered the Ukrainian power grid. The Kyiv attack injured at least two people, Ukraine’s Emergency Service said.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel tells its troops to prepare for a possible ground operation in Lebanon
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel is preparing for a possible ground operation in Lebanon, its army chief said Wednesday as Hezbollah fired dozens of rockets across the border and a missile aimed at Tel Aviv that was the militant group's deepest strike yet.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, TIA GOLDENBERG and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu will address the UN as Israel, bogged down by one war, barrels toward another
item.description: The war in Gaza is about to hit the one-year mark, and Israel is on the cusp of a wider regional war with Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel bombards Hezbollah, killing a top commander, while families flee southern Lebanon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel killed a top Hezbollah commander Tuesday as part of a two-day bombing campaign that has left more than 560 people dead and prompted thousands in southern Lebanon to seek refuge from the widening conflict.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Guterres tells UN meeting that impunity, inequality and other challenges risk engulfing the world
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The head of the United Nations warned gathered leaders Tuesday that impunity, inequality and uncertainty are driving modern civilization toward “a powder keg that risks engulfing the world" — the latest clarion call from Antonio Guterres that the global situation is becoming in
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, NewsNation
